chunks.text: 1 BEFORE THE ELECTION APPELLATE TRIBUNAL FOR SINDH AT SUKKUR Election Appeal No. S-07 of 2024 Syed Amir Ali Shah Jeelani son of Syed Mehar Ali Shah Muslim, Adult by caste Syed R/o H. No. 01-N Ground Floor Block No.2 PECHS Karachi Present Address Saadat House Khairpur Mir’s - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - Appellant V E R S U S 1. Assistant Commissioner/Returning Officer PS-26 Khairpur-I 2. District Election Commissioner, District Khairpur 3. Provincial Election Commissioner of Sindh, Having Office Pakistan Secretariat, Block No. 44-A Shahrah-e-Iraq, Saddar Karachi 4. Election Commission of Pakistan, Through its Secretary G 5/2 Islamabad - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - Respondents Mr. Nazeer Ahmed Chachar, Advocate for appellant Mr. Zeeshan Hyder, Law Officer, Election Commission of Pakistan Mr. Dareshani Ali Hyder ‘Ada’ DAG Mr. Umair Ali Election Officer Mr. Muhammad Ali Returning Officer PS-26 Khairpur-I Date of hearing; 08.01.2024 Date of order; 08.01.2024 O R D E R Zulfiqar Ali Sangi J;- Through this Appeal, the appellant has challenged the impugned order dated 25.12.2023 passed by the Returning Officer PS-26 Khairpur-I whereby his nomination form was rejected on the ground that his proposer was not voter of constituency of the PS-26 Khairpur-I. As per Section 60 of Election Act, 2017 any voter of constituency, may propose or second the name of any qualified person to be a candidate for member of that constituency. The appellant has not moved any application either before the Returning Officer or before this Tribunal for rectification in respect of above defect and such defect is substantial and/or not curable as is held by the Supreme Court

As per Section 60 of Election Act, 2017 any voter of constituency, may propose or second the name of any qualified person to be a candidate for member of that constituency. The appellant has not moved any application either before the Returning Officer or before this Tribunal for rectification in respect of above defect and such defect is substantial and/or not curable as is held by the Supreme Court 2 of Pakistan in the cases of Nadeem Shafi v. Tariq Shuja Butt and others (PLD 2016 Supreme Court 944) and Rana Muhammad Tajammal Hussain v. Raja Shoukat Mehmood (PLD 2007 Supreme Court 277). In view of the above, the fails and is hereby dismissed. J U D G E M.Ali/steno
